title: 1.1: Reports verbose Go test output markers for the SMTP endpoint test run (e.g., lines starting with '=== RUN' or '--- PASS', plus the final 'ok...' line).
1.2: Reports the log line format when a recipient is successfully added (e.g., 'smtp: RCPT ok' with JSON fields such as msg_id and rcpt).
1.3: Reports the log message emitted when an SMTP delivery is aborted mid-transaction (e.g., 'smtp: aborted' with msg_id field).
1.4: Identifies that the module name in the log prefix for SMTP operations is 'smtp'.
1.5: States that the msg_id format is an 8-character lowercase hexadecimal string (e.g., '9a84843e', '91b386b2').
1.6: Reports the sequence of log messages from initial acceptance through retry (e.g., 'queue: delivery attempt failed' followed by 'queue: will retry' followed by 'queue: delivered').
1.7: Identifies the log field that records the scheduled delay before a delivery retry as 'next_try_delay'.
1.8: Reports verbose Go test output markers from running the remote delivery tests (e.g., lines starting with '=== RUN TestRemoteDelivery_AuthMX_Fail' or '=== RUN TestRemoteDelivery_TLSErrFallback').
1.9: Reports the exact error message string when MX authenticity checks fail (e.g., 'Failed to estabilish the MX record (mx.example.invalid.) authenticity').
1.10: Reports the SMTP enhanced status code returned on MX authenticity failure (e.g., 5.4.0 or [5 4 0]).
1.11: Reports the complete reply text returned on MX authenticity failure (e.g., 'No usable MXs, last err: Failed to estabilish the MX record (<mx-host>) authenticity').
1.12: Reports the TLS fallback log message (e.g., 'remote: TLS error, falling back to plaintext' with JSON fields like domain, msg_id, mx, and reason).
1.13: Reports the message arrival log entry (e.g., 'smtp: incoming message' with JSON fields like msg_id, sender, src_host, and src_ip).
